title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22, featuring a mix of Broadway and jazz classics.
The concert celebrated the Boston Pops' 140th season and included performances of iconic American songs like 'Yankee Doodle' and 'A Change is Gonna Come.'
Odom Jr. delivered a powerful rendition of 'Wait for It' from Hamilton, highlighting his Broadway roots and engaging the audience.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, from Chelsea, faces charges for allegedly assaulting two children, including burning and suffocating them.
Prosecutors claim Bautista-Zepeda burned a 2-year-old with a cigarette lighter and a 3-year-old with a heated fork.
Bautista-Zepeda was arrested in Virginia after allegedly fleeing Massachusetts; arraignment held without bail pending a May 28 hearing.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, from Concord, was found guilty of raping a 20-year-old at a Sandwich restaurant in 2023.
Jammal received a 13 ½ to 15 years prison sentence for rape, followed by 5 years probation for assault.
The assault occurred after Jammal followed the victim into a restroom and attacked her despite previous confrontation.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Church of the Open Word in Newtonville will be converted into six condos, with a new building adding 26 units.
Habitech Communities bought the church for $4 million in 2024, after plans for an arts center fell through.
The church redevelopment reflects a trend in Boston where former religious sites are transformed into residential properties.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Celtics may explore trading for Kawhi Leonard, a versatile veteran with championship experience, according to NBA insiders.
LeBron James, entering free agency, could join Boston, providing leadership and versatility at forward or point guard.
John Collins and Jaxson Hayes are potential targets for frontcourt depth; Coby White could bolster guard position if needed.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series by defeating Molloy University in a three-game series.
Endicott College reached the NCAA Division III championship round for the fourth year in a row after beating Salve Regina University.
Bentley's Stan DeMartinis III scored the game-winning run, while Endicott hit three home runs in their decisive victories.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Celtics plan to work out UConn forward Alex Karaban next week before the 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban, a Southborough native, ranks No. 32 on ESPN's top 100 draft prospects list.
Celtics hold the No. 27 and No. 40 picks in the upcoming NBA Draft.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway, blending jazz and pop during his 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour.'
The concert featured both new tracks from Puth's album 'Whatever's Clever!' and older hits, garnering strong audience reactions.
Opening acts Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey showcased their talents, setting the tone for a night of musical excellence.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' and Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' at The Cabot in Beverly.
Jakob Dylan's vocal style has evolved, featuring a gruffer and more expressive tone than during The Wallflowers' '90s heyday.
The concert setlist included hits like 'One Headlight' and 'Three Marlenas,' plus covers of Tom Petty's songs.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston's population declined by 1,338 in the last year, marking the largest decrease in Massachusetts.
Everett gained 2,362 residents, while Worcester added 1,916, reflecting the state's highest population increases.
Massachusetts' population grew by just 0.2 percent last year, due to housing costs and reduced immigration.
